sections.subtitle: Team Form Analysis
sections.text: Torino is struggling with a 4-match winless streak and has failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 4 games. Roma, on the other hand, has shown better form, winning 4 of their last 5 matches, including a strong 3-1 victory against Milan. This suggests Roma has the upper hand in terms of momentum.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Analysis
sections.text: In their last 2 encounters, Roma has dominated with a 1-0 and a 3-2 win over Torino. Roma has not lost to Torino in their last 7 head-to-head matches, often scoring first, which indicates a psychological edge.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics Fine-Tuning
sections.text: Roma ranks higher in the league (5th with 66 points) compared to Torino (11th with 44 points), with a stronger attack (54 goals vs. 39). Torino's defensive stats are decent (7th in least goals conceded), but their lower big chance conversion rate (16th) could hinder them. Roma’s key players like Paulo Dybala remain a significant threat.

sections.subtitle: Match Dynamics and Key Factors
sections.text: Roma tends to score early (often first to score in head-to-heads and recent matches), while Torino struggles defensively in early phases. The referee’s average of 4.8 yellow cards per game aligns with both teams’ physical playstyle, and corner stats suggest a moderate total around recent averages.
